item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1960s_GEC_New_Gecophone_706_Black_Rotary_Dial_Telephone.JPG

CC-BY-SA 3.0

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image can be sourced back to here, which holds all content under an All Rights Reserved license. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and is pending staff evaualtion for removal/replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love SCPs that are/were Foundation scientists because they bring a new level of personality and depth to the article. This one in particular has such a cool concept, and I think what really brings it home for me is Dr. [REDACTED]'s obvious enthusiasm for research. Something about an eager scientist becoming the very thing he studied is just such a fun concept, and I like how it's pulled off here.

My only wish (which I have no expectations will be indulged, it's just a personal thought) is that there was a little more in this article from the doctor himself, like an interview at least. His personality is what really pulls this together for me, and I'd love to see more of it however possible. That said, the article absolutely stands just fine as it is and I consider it one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I DEMAND THE ABILITY TO UPVOTE COMMENTS.
GIMME.
NOW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would LOVE to see some expanded addenda on this featuring SCP-86's musings, propsitions, and beliefs concerning its nature and origin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Halfway through this re-read I started interpreting "Dr. [REDACTED]" not as censorship but rather as his name. As in "Hi, my name is John Redacted."

Which would imply a backstory. Our subject was clearly a devoted and diligent employee. He took security procedures seriously. Before his untimely "demise"; so many reports had the comment "Nonessential sensitive information removed by order of Dr. Redacted" that the clerical staff started using his name as shorthand for security edits in general. And that, kiddies, is where the word redacted comes from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually really like this one's idea! (Not to imply that it's not well executed, quite the opposite. Most of the time, I'm neutral on the idea and the way it's written helps, but this one would be awesome to me most ways it could be written.) Having a doctor die, then their office apparently being them…
Also, eating staples…
that's one way of getting your iron.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
As author you of course always have the final say, so feel free to veto. Its a real trick to suggest changes to already super successful articles, since they tend to be successful for a reason.

+1 BTW.

Edit: Given the reaction to the proposal, at the current time its safer to err on the side of no change. Timer has been removed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hm. That's… not necessarily an invalid suggestion, but I'm not sure I like it. Let me think about it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer removed upon expiration. No edits made (see below.)

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

The acquisition log adds plenty of opportunity for wikiwalking, requiring something that could transfer consciousness to officer furniture and make office furniture out of tissue. I suggest keeping SCP-Blackbox since that keeps a lot of the mystery that makes this SCP neat, and stating that rather during its decommissioning it was during crosstesting with SCP-1415 an object that certainly has the capability to have inanimate objects formed suddenly out of tissue.

Editing this passage:

SCP-086 is presumed to have been created on June 12, 1978, when Dr. [REDACTED] was presumed killed by the PN-class events resulting from the decommissioning of SCP-████. On June 20, 1978, Dr. [REDACTED]'s office was being emptied by maintenance personnel so that it could be assigned to his successor. At this point, SCP-086-1 began ringing repeatedly, despite not being connected to a telephone jack. Maintenance personnel notified level-3 operatives, who answered SCP-086-1.

To read:

SCP-086 is presumed to have been created on June 12, 1978, when Dr. [REDACTED] was presumed killed by the PN-class events resulting from the destruction of SCP-████ following a series of cross tests with SCP-1415. On June 20, 1978, Dr. [REDACTED]'s office was being emptied by maintenance personnel so that it could be assigned to his successor. At this point, SCP-086-1 began ringing repeatedly, despite not being connected to a telephone jack. Maintenance personnel notified level-3 operatives, who answered SCP-086-1.

In this sense the sudden appearance of tissue furniture is given a bit of an explanation, yet we are left with the mystery of what kind of kooky tests were going down, and what exactly was SCP-Blackbox.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded:
+1

That should help fix it up a bit.

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded: PARTY POOPERS, THE LOT OF YA

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded: With 6 votes from deleted accounts, it's actually +294 right now…
Damn fine SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this one is an interesting and three-dimensional character, and the SCP also invokes a feeling of mystery.

On one hand, the SCP could actually a malevolent entity posing as the mind of the doctor, and is playing the most amazing game of reverse psychology ever.

On the other hand, it could be the imprint of the doctor's person on his own office, caused by the office "imprinting" on him, or something else.

On the other other hand, if I woke up one day and found that I was turned into all my stuff, telling everybody that I'm probably not actually me and am most likely a malevolent and lying force pretending to be me is something I would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jeez, talk about living in your office…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay yeah, this is p funny ha ha living phone and all that, but then you think about how worried he seems about accidentally ruining things ( being cooperative and all ) and its actually really SAD??? I'm feeling emotional over a bunch of office supplies someone save me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. Why can I only upvote once?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea of personell contacting superiors to *answer a telephone*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: May I direct you to SCP-2800 and my own SCP-2273? Both get on with the Foundation just fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this SCP is implied to have once been SCP staff. It's worried about itself doing something terrible, so it keeps trying to push for more containment.

It's pretty sad, really. Poor guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got a soft spot for skips that choose of their own free will to peacefully cooperate with the Foundation. This doesn't seem to happen that much anymore these days.

Now I feel like an elderly hippie. :/

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this, but it's not enough to stop me from upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one but usage of [REDACTED] for the name instead of just blacking it out as usual rubs me the wrong way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the catchy, humorous nature of him being called Dr. [REDACTED] but it doesn't really make any sense when you give the date of his death as well as other info. That quibble aside, this is great. It reminds me of the kind of weirdness they'd have in Lexx, actually.
Drinks all around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is freaking hilarious, but not funny in a -J kind of way. I was already looking to +1 it, but the whole turning himself in thing, and arguing to be Euclid… lol those sealed the deal. Awesome edit there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was one of the articles that got me hooked on the Foundation. I love every part of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers. xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I was reading that -2, -3, and -4 are how it eats, I thought to myself, "So that must mean -5 is…OH MY!". So glad the featured tale brought me to this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was pleasantly indifferent to this one right up until I read the part where he turned himself in as either being a new SCP. And then I wanted to upvote it many times. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Dr. [REDACTED]. It is so deliciously objective.

…I feel like a comment of this nature has been made already…

In any case, happy 100, Dr. [REDACTED].

This is interesting to think about. To be an SCP and have knowledge of the Foundation, and to know that inherently as an SCP one could be quite dangerous, but warning personnel would result in potentially more restrictive containment procedures, and there is still the wish to aid the Foundation if possible, but that help would be limited or refused…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy, funny and sad, all at the same time. Well done. This is one of my favorites now.
Currently envisioning some groovy 70's scientists wiping the 'This Site Has Worked __ Days Without A Lost Time Accident' -sign clean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In-canon of the SCP, there would be no reason that the object class would ever include Euclid. It was recommended but denied. If anything, it'd be "Safe Euclid Safe"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh, I can't upvote this harder. This thing is amazing with the rewrite, great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the original, but this rewrite is fantastic. Everything works great. Why can't I upvote more?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically they're not microfiche. They used to give 086 microfiche to eat, but their microfiche suppliers have gone out of business.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted with considerable glee. I like it. Even the water cooler. And the microfiche/staples/shavings are a darn nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, I'm thinking about what to put in place of the EXPUNGED. No decision yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation: the only place when the employee of the month is a sapient telephone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very much improved. If I could just make one suggestion, it would be to remove the [DATA EXPUNGED] from the description. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This rewrite is really good. One of the funniest non -Js on the site.

item.title: Re: ITYM Upvoted
item.content:encoded:
Oh god! OH GOD!! IT'S BROOOOOOKKKKKEEEENNNNN!!!!

(+1)

item.title: Re: ITYM Upvoted
item.content:encoded:
haha no… that's just my brain not working.

Drewbear's trackpad will break in the future. This is an absolute certainty.

item.title: ITYM Upvoted
item.content:encoded:
I think you meant that you upvoted this so hard that you broke your own keyboard in the process (evidenced by the typo).

I really like this rewrite. The execution of the concept is solid and it's nice to have something similar to SCP-002 with a different twist.

Somebody needs to play around with the calculator and also type in silly things just to see what happens. 0.7734, 5318008, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have updated upvoted this so hard that Drewbear's trackpad will break (at an unspecified point in the future)

edit DERP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rewrite absolutely tickles me pink. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …. ear / mouth, yes. Oops. Thanks, I'll fix that now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's a whole hell of a LOT better now. i really, really like that the scp actually has a metabolism, which (to my mind) makes it worlds better than most of the entries around here.

two things jumped out at me:

a: shouldn't the mouth be in the ear piece, and the ear be in the mouthpiece? otherwise anyone talking directly (rather than remotely) to 086 has to hold the handset upside down.

b: storing waste products in the water cooler is seriously D: perhaps waste could appear in a standard old school trash can, and the water cooler takes water as input? put a new tank on every month, works as normal except for what 086 drinks, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's better. I still don't like it. I feel the concept has been done to death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I normally like Voct rewrites, but this one's not doing anything for me
It just feels like the ideas have been done before.
Sorry.

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded:
i like this, especially if turning him into a full set of office equipment can also provide him with *some* sort of ecology.

though i do find the idea of a high level researcher/SCP administrator willfully relegating himself to only processing mundane things hard to swallow, especially if he was still making decisions in a crisis.

passing thought: surely there's enough possessed furniture and equipment scattered throughout the foundation to make a whole office suite already… though, admittedly, it would probably be an evil office suite. hmm

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I say let him write a new one, and let's delete this one.

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded: I will upvote this gleefully. :D

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of the whole room being part of him. I look forward to this.

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded:
He's very conscientious. He says "no! I don't qualify for a security clearance any more! You don't know what I really am, only what I claim to be!"

I'm gonna change some of the details. I'm moving the [REDACTED] incident to the 1970s, also.

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded: I am okay with this, I would point out that he only continued to act as administrator during the immediate crisis in 1938. After that he functioned as a consulting zoologist. I had left the 04 clearance due to his previous knowledge and I have not seen demotions used in the Foundation that were merely a clearance downgrade.

item.title: Re: Notes on a rewrite
item.content:encoded:
This is a neat idea indeed.
Seconding what Drewbear said.
